executive_summary: With the conclusion of the National Two Sessions, expectations for innovative drug reimbursement policies are heating up; GLP-1 drugs have entered a critical window for NRDL negotiation renewal, with the industry focused on balancing price maintenance and volume expansion

Competition between Eli Lilly's tirzepatide and Novo Nordisk's semaglutide has intensified, with the narrative focus shifting from 'weight loss percentage' to long-term management issues such as 'muscle preservation' and 'rebound after discontinuation'

Innovent's mazdutide (Xinermei) is in the early post-launch volume expansion phase, demonstrating accessibility advantages under the domestic substitution narrative, but facing challenges in capacity ramp-up and channel construction

NMPA released the 'Annual Report on Adverse Reaction Monitoring of GLP-1 Receptor Agonist Drugs', with medullary thyroid carcinoma risk and gastrointestinal reactions becoming key focuses of regulatory warnings
Pfizer's oral GLP-1 (enugrutide) marketing application was accepted, with the convenience advantage triggering market discussion on adherence bottlenecks of injectable formulations
industry_overview: In March 2026, the GLP-1 weight loss sector entered a rational adjustment phase following the post-boom period. At the regulatory level, based on accumulated post-market adverse reaction data, the NMPA strengthened risk management requirements for GLP-1 drugs, requiring companies to prominently display thyroid C-cell tumor risk warnings in prescribing information. At the market level, as tirzepatide and semaglutide have been launched in China for one year, medical insurance negotiation renewals have become a core issue, with downward price pressure and improved patient accessibility forming a complex trade-off. In terms of public perception, discussions on UGC platforms have shifted from "influencer weight loss drug experience sharing" to "long-term medical weight management," with significantly rising attention to side effects such as "rebound after discontinuation," "facial aging (Ozempic Face)," and "muscle loss." Additionally, during the 2026 National Two Sessions, the three-year action plan for "Weight Management Year" was further implemented, accelerating the establishment of weight management clinics in public hospitals and driving GLP-1 drugs' transition from consumer healthcare back to serious healthcare.
